sp_widget.description: Custom unauthenticated Service Portal widget for case submission. Renders a 5-field form (subject, type, description, requester_name, requester_email) and POSTs to /api/x_casemgmt/case_submit on Submit. On success, embeds the confirmation widget (sp_widget_x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget) via $sp.getWidget() and the <sp-widget> directive, passing the auto-generated case number through c.data.confirmationWidget.options.number so the embedded widget renders the verbatim message 'Your case has been submitted' plus the case number per AAP Section 0.4.4. On any HTTP error or missing-number response, displays a generic 'Submission failed. Please try again.' alert (no server diagnostics leaked). Embedded by sp_page_x_casemgmt_case_submit. The 5-field whitelist is enforced at three layers (form inputs, payload construction, and Script Include) for defense-in-depth. Replaces ArkCase's acm-service-portal-gateway DefaultPluggablePortalRequestService.submitRequest pattern. No client-side or server-side database operations - all writes flow through the scripted REST endpoint. Per AAP Sections 0.4.3, 0.4.4, 0.5.1, and 0.7.4 (verbatim 'Your case has been submitted' message lives in the embedded confirmation widget) and Validation Gate 4 (Portal - submission).
sp_widget.name: Case Submission Widget
sp_widget.script: (function() { /* * Case Submission Widget - server-side data initialization. * * The server script's sole responsibility is to pre-load the * embedded confirmation widget reference and initialize the * submission state flags so the client controller has a valid * c.data object on first render. * * Per AAP Section 0.7.4 / Phase 7 of the agent prompt: * - data.confirmationWidget is loaded via $sp.getWidget() by * stable widget ID (no hardcoded sys_id). The empty initial * number is a placeholder; the client-side success callback * mutates c.data.confirmationWidget.options.number after the * POST returns. * - data.submitted is initialized to false so the template's * ng-if="!c.data.submitted" expression evaluates true on * first render and the form panel is shown. * - data.submittedNumber is initialized to '' (empty string) * so the template can safely interpolate c.data.submittedNumber * even before a successful submission has occurred (no "null" * or "undefined" rendering). * * This widget intentionally performs ZERO database operations: * - No GlideRecord reads (no fetching of case data; the widget * renders with empty form state and the user drives the * submission via the form). * - No GlideRecord writes (all writes happen through the * scripted REST endpoint at sys_ws_definition_x_casemgmt_case_submit * plus its sys_ws_operation_x_casemgmt_case_submit_post handler; * this widget does NOT duplicate that contract). * - No gs.eventQueue() calls (email notifications are disabled * per AAP Section 0.7.2 Email-disabled constraint). * * The IIFE pattern matches ServiceNow's standard widget server- * script convention. The implicit `data` and `$sp` symbols are * provided by the Service Portal framework at execution time. */ data.confirmationWidget = $sp.getWidget('x_casemgmt_case_confirmation_widget', { number: '' }); data.submitted = false; data.submittedNumber = ''; })();
sp_widget.template:
Submit a Case

{{c.errorMessage}}
